content: The lexical ambiguity of "reflexion" spanning both general human cognitive reflection and the specialized AI agent framework can be leveraged to design a pedagogical LLM self-reflection mechanism for high school English writing workshops that trains both student metacognition and model self-improvement simultaneously. This dual-purpose system uses student writing reflections as human supervision data to fine-tune the LLM's own self-reflection module, turning a standard language learning activity into a collaborative improvement loop.
